The other BERT model guides the crossover of individual genes by predicting the crossover point. Experimental results demonstrate that GESR significantly improves computational efficiency compared with traditional GP algorithms and achieves strong overall performance across multiple symbolic regression tasks.

analysis.tldr: 利用BERT模型作为基因编辑工具指导遗传编程中的变异和交叉，显著提升符号回归效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 遗传编程（GP）在符号回归中虽经典，但变异和交叉完全随机，产生大量无效变异，导致进化效率低下。
analysis.problem: 如何通过有指导的基因编辑替代随机变异和交叉，提高遗传编程在符号回归中的搜索效率。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 训练两个BERT模型分别指导基因变异（利用掩码语言建模）和交叉点预测，实现定向基因编辑。
contributions: 提出基于基因编辑的符号回归方法GESR，用BERT模型指导GP的变异和交叉。
实验证明GESR相比传统GP显著提升计算效率，并在多个符号回归任务上取得强整体性能。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 依赖BERT模型的训练质量，可能对数据量敏感。
基因编辑的指导可能限制搜索多样性，导致陷入局部最优。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 利用BERT模型作为基因编辑工具指导遗传编程中的变异和交叉，显著提升符号回归效率。

papers.title: Evolving-RL: End-to-End Optimization of Experience-Driven Self-Evolving Capability within Agents
papers.abstract: Experience-driven self-evolving agents aim to overcome the static nature of large language models by distilling reusable experience from past interactions, thus enabling adaptation to novel tasks at deployment time. This process places substantial demands on the foundation model's capacities for abstraction, generalization, and in-context learning. However, most existing studies focus primarily on system-level design choices, such as how experience is represented and managed, neglecting the inherent capabilities of the underlying model. While some recent works have started to optimize the experience utilization stage via reinforcement learning, they still fail to treat self-evolution as a unified process to be jointly optimized. To this end, we propose Evolving-RL, an efficient algorithmic framework that jointly improves the experience extraction and utilization capabilities required for self-evolution. Specifically, we center the learning process on experience extraction and evaluation, using the two supervisory signals derived from evaluation to optimize the extractor and solver separately and thus enable their coordinated co-evolution. Experiments on ALFWorld and Mind2Web show that Evolving-RL effectively enhances LLMs' ability to extract and reuse experience, leading to strong performance gains on out-of-distribution tasks (up to 98.7% relative improvement over the GRPO baseline on ALFWorld unseen tasks and 35.8% on Mind2Web), and these gains are fully unlocked only through the coordinated co-evolution of experience extraction and utilization. Furthermore, Evolving-RL inherently functions as an experience-augmented RL algorithm. By internalizing reusable experience patterns directly into model parameters, it achieves remarkable performance gains over standard baselines on both seen and unseen tasks, even in the absence of test-time experience accumulation.

analysis.tldr: 提出Evolving-RL框架，通过强化学习联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，实现端到端的自我进化。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有经验驱动型自我进化智能体主要关注系统级设计（如经验表示与管理），忽视了底层模型的内在能力；虽有工作通过强化学习优化经验利用阶段，但未将自我进化视为统一过程进行联合优化。
analysis.problem: 如何通过强化学习端到端地联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，以实现高效的自我进化。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出Evolving-RL框架，以经验提取和评估为中心，利用评估产生的两个监督信号分别优化提取器和求解器，实现两者的协调共进化。
contributions: 提出Evolving-RL，首个端到端联合优化经验提取与利用的强化学习框架。
在ALFWorld和Mind2Web上验证了有效性，在分布外任务上相对GRPO基线提升高达98.7%和35.8%。
analysis.experiments: 在ALFWorld和Mind2Web上进行实验，结果显示Evolving-RL有效提升LLM的经验提取与复用能力，在分布外任务上取得显著性能提升。
limitations: 可能依赖任务环境设计，泛化性需进一步验证。
框架复杂度较高，训练开销可能较大。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出Evolving-RL框架，通过强化学习联合优化智能体的经验提取与利用能力，实现端到端的自我进化。

papers.title: diffGHOST: Diffusion based Generative Hedged Oblivious Synthetic Trajectories
papers.abstract: Trajectories are nowadays valuable information for a wide range of applications. However they are also inherently sensitive, as they contain highly personal information about individuals. Facing this challenge, synthesizing mobility trajectories has emerged as a promising solution to leverage mobility information while preserving privacy. State-of-the-art models, often rely on the false assumptions of generative models implicit privacy and fails to provide privacy guarantees while preserving trajectories utility. Here, we introduce diffGHOST, a conditional diffusion model based on latent space segmentation, designed to answer this challenge. Thus, this paper propose a methodology that identify and mitigate memorization of critical samples using condition segments of a learn latent space.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于潜空间分割的条件扩散模型 diffGHOST，用于生成具有隐私保证的合成轨迹。
analysis.research_motivation: 轨迹数据包含高度个人信息，直接使用存在隐私风险；现有生成模型常隐含假设隐私保护，但缺乏可证明的隐私保证，且难以平衡轨迹效用与隐私。
analysis.problem: 如何在生成合成轨迹时提供可量化的隐私保证（如差分隐私），同时保持轨迹的实用性和生成质量。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 现有生成模型隐式假设隐私保护，但实际存在记忆关键样本的风险，导致隐私泄露。
analysis.method: 提出 diffGHOST，一种基于潜空间分割的条件扩散模型，通过识别和缓解对关键样本的记忆来提供隐私保证。
contributions: 提出基于潜空间分割的条件扩散模型 diffGHOST，用于合成轨迹生成。
提出一种识别和缓解关键样本记忆的方法，提供隐私保证。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能仅在特定轨迹数据集上验证，泛化性未知。
隐私与效用之间的权衡可能未充分探索。
analysis.primary_area_en: generative models
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于潜空间分割的条件扩散模型 diffGHOST，用于生成具有隐私保证的合成轨迹。

papers.title: Navigating the Sea of LLM Evaluation: Investigating Bias in Toxicity Benchmarks
papers.abstract: The rapid adoption of LLMs in both research and industry highlights the challenges of deploying them safely and reveals a gap in the systematic evaluation of toxicity benchmarks. As organizations increasingly rely on these benchmarks to certify models for customer-facing applications and automated moderation, unrecognized evaluation biases could lead to the deployment of vulnerable or unsafe systems. This work investigates the robustness of established benchmarking setups and examines how to measure currently neglected intrinsic biases, such as those related to model choice, metrics, and task types. Our experiments uncover significant discrepancies in benchmark behaviors when evaluation setups are altered. Specifically, shifting the task from text completion to summarization increases the tendency of benchmarks to flag content as harmful. Additionally, certain benchmarks fail to maintain consistent behavior when the input data domain is changed. Furthermore, we observe model-specific instabilities, demonstrating a clear need for more robust and comprehensive safety evaluation frameworks.

analysis.tldr: 发现毒性基准在任务类型、数据域和模型选择上存在显著偏差，需更稳健的安全评估框架。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM在研究和工业中快速部署，但毒性基准的系统性评估存在空白，未识别的评估偏差可能导致不安全系统上线。
analysis.problem: 现有毒性基准在评估设置（任务类型、数据域、模型选择）改变时表现出不一致性，且缺乏对内在偏差的测量。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 实验发现：将任务从文本补全改为摘要时，基准更易将内容标记为有害；某些基准在输入数据域变化时行为不一致；模型特定不稳定性存在。
analysis.method: 通过系统改变评估设置（任务类型、数据域、模型选择）来测量基准的鲁棒性和内在偏差。
contributions: 揭示了毒性基准在任务类型、数据域和模型选择上的显著偏差
强调了需要更稳健和全面的安全评估框架
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能未覆盖所有类型的毒性基准或任务
未提出具体的偏差缓解方法
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 发现毒性基准在任务类型、数据域和模型选择上存在显著偏差，需更稳健的安全评估框架。

papers.title: Teacher-Aware Evolution of Heuristic Programs from Learned Optimization Policies
papers.abstract: LLM-based automatic heuristic design has shown promise for generating executable heuristics for combinatorial optimization, but existing methods mainly rely on delayed endpoint performance. We propose a \emph{teacher-aware evolutionary framework} that uses independently trained learned optimization policies as behavioral teachers. Instead of deploying or imitating the teacher, our method queries it on states visited by candidate heuristic programs and uses its action preferences as local feedback for evolution. The resulting search discovers static executable heuristics guided by both task performance and teacher-derived behavioral signals. Experiments on scheduling, routing, and graph optimization benchmarks show that our method improves over performance-driven LLM heuristic evolution baselines while requiring no neural inference at deployment. These results suggest that learned optimization policies can be repurposed as behavioral feedback sources for automatic heuristic discovery.

analysis.tldr: 提出教师感知进化框架，利用学习优化策略的行为偏好引导启发式程序进化，无需部署教师模型。
analysis.research_motivation: 基于LLM的自动启发式设计虽有前景，但现有方法主要依赖延迟的终点性能，缺乏中间行为指导，导致搜索效率低。
analysis.problem: 如何利用已训练的学习优化策略作为行为教师，在不部署或模仿教师的情况下，为启发式程序进化提供局部反馈。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出教师感知进化框架：独立训练学习优化策略作为行为教师，在候选启发式程序访问的状态上查询教师，将其动作偏好作为局部反馈用于进化搜索，最终发现静态可执行启发式。
contributions: 提出教师感知进化框架，利用学习优化策略的行为偏好作为局部反馈，无需部署教师模型。
在调度、路由和图优化基准上，该方法优于仅依赖性能驱动的LLM启发式进化基线。
analysis.experiments: 在调度、路由和图优化基准上进行了实验，结果显示方法优于性能驱动的LLM启发式进化基线，且部署时无需神经网络推理。
limitations: 可能依赖教师策略的质量和覆盖范围。
未讨论教师策略训练成本或跨任务泛化能力。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出教师感知进化框架，利用学习优化策略的行为偏好引导启发式程序进化，无需部署教师模型。

papers.title: Hierarchical Causal Abduction: A Foundation Framework for Explainable Model Predictive Control
papers.abstract: Model Predictive Control (MPC) is widely used to operate safety-critical infrastructure by predicting future trajectories and optimizing control actions. However, nonlinear dynamics, hard safety constraints, and numerical optimization often render individual control moves opaque to human operators, undermining trust and hindering deployment. This paper presents Hierarchical Causal Abduction (HCA), which combines (i) physics-informed reasoning via domain knowledge graphs, (ii) optimization evidence from Karush--Kuhn--Tucker (KKT) multipliers, and (iii) temporal causal discovery via the PCMCI algorithm to generate faithful, human-interpretable explanations for control actions computed by nonlinear MPC. Across three diverse control applications (greenhouse climate, building HVAC, chemical process engineering) with expert validation, HCA improves explanation accuracy by 53\% over LIME (0.478 vs. 0.311) using a single set of cross-domain parameters without per-domain tuning; domain-specific KKT-threshold calibration over 2--3 days further increases accuracy to 0.88. Ablation studies confirm that each evidence source is essential, with 32--37\% accuracy degradation when any component is removed, and HCA's ranking-and-validation methodology generalizes beyond MPC to other prediction-based decision systems, including learning-based control and trajectory planning.

analysis.tldr: 提出分层因果溯因框架（HCA），融合物理知识图谱、KKT乘子和因果发现，为非线性模型预测控制生成可解释性说明，准确率显著优于LIME。
analysis.research_motivation: 非线性MPC在安全关键基础设施中广泛应用，但其控制动作因非线性动力学、硬约束和数值优化而难以解释，削弱了操作员信任并阻碍部署。
analysis.problem: 如何为非线性MPC计算的控制动作生成忠实、人类可理解的解释。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出分层因果溯因（HCA），结合物理知识图谱的物理推理、KKT乘子的优化证据和PCMCI算法的时间因果发现，生成解释。
contributions: 提出HCA框架，融合三种证据源为非线性MPC提供可解释性。
在三个控制应用上验证，HCA准确率比LIME提升53%，且无需跨域调参。
analysis.experiments: 在温室气候、建筑HVAC、化学过程工程三个控制应用上，经专家验证，HCA准确率0.478（LIME 0.311），域特定KKT阈值校准后达0.88；消融实验显示移除任一组件准确率下降32-37%。
limitations: 可能依赖领域知识图谱的构建质量。
KKT阈值校准需2-3天，可能限制快速部署。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出分层因果溯因框架（HCA），融合物理知识图谱、KKT乘子和因果发现，为非线性模型预测控制生成可解释性说明，准确率显著优于LIME。

papers.title: PRISM: Generation-Time Detection and Mitigation of Secret Leakage in Multi-Agent LLM Pipelines
papers.abstract: Multi-agent LLM systems introduce a security risk in which sensitive information accessed by one agent can propagate through shared context and reappear in downstream outputs, even without explicit adversarial intent. We formalise this phenomenon as propagation amplification, where leakage risk increases across agent boundaries as sensitive content is repeatedly exposed to downstream generators. Existing defences, including prompt-based safeguards, static pattern matching, and LLM-as-judge filtering, are not designed for this setting: they either operate after generation, rely primarily on surface-form patterns, or add substantial latency without modelling the generation process itself. To resolve these issues, we propose PRISM, a real-time defence that treats credential leakage as a sequential risk accumulation problem during generation. At each decoding step, PRISM combines 16 signals spanning lexical, structural, information-theoretic, behavioural, and contextual features into a calibrated risk score, enabling per-token intervention through green, yellow, and red risk zones. Our central observation is that credential reproduction is often preceded by a measurable shift in generation dynamics, characterised by entropy collapse and increasing logit concentration. When combined with text-structural cues such as identifier-pattern detection, these temporal signals provide an early warning of leakage before a secret is fully reconstructed. Across a 2,000-task adversarial benchmark covering 13 attack categories and three pressure levels in a heterogeneous four-agent pipeline, PRISM achieves F1 = 0.832 with precision = 1.000 and recall = 0.712, while producing no observed leakage on our benchmark (0.0% task-level leak rate) and preserving output utility of 0.893. It substantially outperforms the strongest baseline, Span Tagger, which achieves F1 = 0.719 with a 15.0% task-level leak rate.

analysis.tldr: PRISM 在生成过程中实时检测并阻止多智能体 LLM 流水线中的秘密泄露，通过 16 维信号和风险分区实现高精度零泄露。
analysis.research_motivation: 多智能体 LLM 系统中，敏感信息可在智能体间通过共享上下文传播并出现在下游输出中，现有防御（提示过滤、模式匹配、LLM 评判）无法有效应对这种传播放大风险。
analysis.problem: 多智能体 LLM 流水线中秘密泄露的实时检测与缓解问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 秘密重现前常出现生成动态的可测量变化，表现为熵坍缩和 logit 集中度增加，结合文本结构线索可提前预警。
analysis.method: PRISM 将泄露视为生成过程中的序列风险累积，每步解码结合 16 个信号（词汇、结构、信息论、行为、上下文）计算校准风险分数，通过绿/黄/红区域进行逐 token 干预。
contributions: 形式化定义了多智能体 LLM 系统中的传播放大现象
提出 PRISM，一种实时、逐 token 的泄露检测与缓解方法
在 2000 任务对抗基准上实现 F1=0.832，零泄露率
analysis.experiments: 在包含 13 种攻击类别、三种压力水平的 2000 任务对抗基准上评估，使用异构四智能体流水线。PRISM 达到 F1=0.832（精确率 1.000，召回率 0.712），任务级泄露率 0.0%，输出效用 0.893，显著优于基线 Span Tagger（F1=0.719，泄露率 15.0%）。
limitations: 可能依赖特定类型的秘密（如凭证）特征，对其他类型敏感信息效果未知
实时干预可能影响生成流畅性，尽管效用分数较高
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: PRISM 在生成过程中实时检测并阻止多智能体 LLM 流水线中的秘密泄露，通过 16 维信号和风险分区实现高精度零泄露。

papers.title: The Open-Box Fallacy: Why AI Deployment Needs a Calibrated Verification Regime
papers.abstract: AI deployment in sensitive domains such as health care, credit, employment, and criminal justice is often treated as unsafe to authorize until model internals can be explained. This often leads to an excessive reliance on mechanistic interpretability to address a deployment challenge beyond its intended scope. We argue that the gate should instead be calibrated verification: authorization should be domain-scoped, independently checkable, monitored after release, accountable, contestable, and revocable. The reason is twofold. First, model capability is uneven across nearby tasks, so authorization must attach to a specific use rather than to a model in general. Second, societies have long governed opaque expertise through credentials, monitoring, liability, appeal, and revocation rather than mechanism-level explanation. Recent evidence reinforces this distinction between mechanistic understanding and deployment authority: a 53-percentage-point gap between internal representations and output correction shows that understanding may not translate into action, while one scoping review found that only 9.0% of FDA-approved AI/ML device documents contained a prospective post-market surveillance study. We propose Verification Coverage, a six-component reportable standard with a minimum-composition rule, as the metric that should sit beside capability scores in model cards, leaderboards, and regulatory disclosures.

analysis.tldr: AI部署不应依赖机制可解释性，而应转向领域限定、可独立核查的校准验证机制。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前AI部署在医疗、信贷等敏感领域常因无法解释模型内部机制而被视为不安全，导致过度依赖机制可解释性来解决超出其范围的部署挑战。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一个更有效的AI部署授权机制，替代对机制可解释性的过度依赖。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 模型能力在相近任务间不均匀，授权必须针对具体用途而非通用模型；社会长期通过凭证、监控、责任、申诉和撤销来管理不透明专业知识，而非机制级解释；内部表征与输出修正之间存在53个百分点的差距，表明理解不一定转化为行动；仅9.0%的FDA批准的AI/ML设备文档包含前瞻性上市后监测研究。
analysis.method: 提出校准验证（Calibrated Verification）框架，包括领域限定、独立可核查、发布后监控、可问责、可争议和可撤销六个要素，并引入验证覆盖率（Verification Coverage）作为六分量可报告标准。
contributions: 论证了AI部署中过度依赖机制可解释性的问题，提出校准验证作为替代授权范式。
提出验证覆盖率（Verification Coverage）作为模型卡、排行榜和监管披露中的新指标。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 验证覆盖率的具体实施细节和有效性尚需进一步实证研究。
可能面临监管和行业采纳的阻力。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: AI部署不应依赖机制可解释性，而应转向领域限定、可独立核查的校准验证机制。

papers.title: Budget-Efficient Automatic Algorithm Design via Code Graph
papers.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have emerged as powerful tools for automatic algorithm design (AAD). However, existing pipelines remain inefficient. They operate at the granularity of full algorithms, redundantly rewriting recurring substructures and discarding low-fitness candidates that may contain valuable algorithmic features. We formalize budget-efficient automatic algorithm design, wherein the search policy maximizes realized fitness subject to limited computational cost. We propose a directed acyclic graph representation of algorithms and build a search framework that fully exploits the LLM's output. Instead of querying the LLM for full algorithms, we use it to obtain corrections: compact operators that add, replace, or remove code blocks. Each correction augments the graph, yielding new algorithms that compose with prior corrections. This graph structure decomposes algorithms into sets of corrections, enabling correction-level credit assignment that informs subsequent queries. We complement this framework with theoretical insights into the ideal balance between search depth and breadth at different budget levels. We validate our method empirically on three combinatorial optimization problems, demonstrating consistent superiority of our graph-based search over full-algorithm search at equal token budget. Finally, our experiments suggest that rich contexts help only when the LLM's prior knowledge is shallow, and can hinder performance otherwise.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于代码图的预算高效自动算法设计方法，通过增量修正替代全算法搜索，提升搜索效率。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有LLM驱动的自动算法设计方法效率低下，以完整算法为粒度重复重写重复子结构，并丢弃可能含有有价值特征的低适应度候选解，导致计算资源浪费。
analysis.problem: 如何在有限计算预算下最大化自动算法设计中的适应度，即预算高效的自动算法设计问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 将算法表示为有向无环图，利用LLM生成修正操作（添加、替换或删除代码块）来增量更新图结构，实现修正级别的信用分配，并理论分析不同预算下搜索深度与广度的平衡。
contributions: 形式化定义了预算高效的自动算法设计问题。
提出基于代码图的搜索框架，通过增量修正替代全算法搜索，实现修正级信用分配。
理论分析了不同预算下搜索深度与广度的最优平衡。
在三个组合优化问题上验证了方法优于全算法搜索。
analysis.experiments: 在三个组合优化问题上进行实验，表明基于图的搜索在相同token预算下一致优于全算法搜索；实验还发现丰富上下文仅在LLM先验知识浅时有益，否则可能阻碍性能。
limitations: 方法依赖LLM生成修正的质量，可能受限于LLM能力。
代码图表示可能不适用于所有算法结构。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于代码图的预算高效自动算法设计方法，通过增量修正替代全算法搜索，提升搜索效率。

papers.title: LLARS: Enabling Domain Expert & Developer Collaboration for LLM Prompting, Generation and Evaluation
papers.abstract: We demonstrate LLARS (LLM Assisted Research System), an open-source platform that bridges the gap between domain experts and developers for building LLM-based systems. It integrates three tightly connected modules into an end-to-end pipeline: Collaborative Prompt Engineering for real-time co-authoring with version control and instant LLM testing, Batch Generation for configurable output production across user-selected prompts $\times$ models $\times$ data with cost control, and Hybrid Evaluation where human and LLM evaluators jointly assess outputs through diverse assessment methods, with live agreement metrics and provenance analysis to identify the best model-prompt combination for a given use case. New prompts and models are automatically available for batch generation and completed batches can be turned into evaluation scenarios with a single click. Interviews with six domain experts and three developers in online counselling confirmed that LLARS feels intuitive, saves considerable time by keeping everything in one place and makes interdisciplinary collaboration seamless.

analysis.tldr: LLARS是一个开源平台，通过协作提示工程、批量生成和混合评估模块，促进领域专家与开发者共同构建LLM系统。
analysis.research_motivation: 构建LLM系统时，领域专家和开发者之间缺乏有效的协作工具，导致沟通成本高、工作流程碎片化。
analysis.problem: 如何设计一个集成化平台，支持领域专家和开发者协同进行LLM提示工程、生成和评估。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出LLARS平台，包含三个模块：协作提示工程（实时协作、版本控制、即时测试）、批量生成（可配置输出、成本控制）、混合评估（人类与LLM评估者联合评估，提供一致性指标和溯源分析）。
contributions: 提出一个开源平台LLARS，集成提示工程、批量生成和混合评估的端到端流水线。
通过在线咨询领域的专家访谈验证了平台的直观性、效率提升和跨学科协作的流畅性。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果，仅提及对六位领域专家和三位开发者的访谈。
limitations: 平台可能仅适用于特定领域（如在线咨询），泛化性未验证。
混合评估中LLM评估者的可靠性可能受限于评估任务。

papers.title: A Resilient Solution for Sewer Overflow Monitoring across Cloud and Edge
papers.abstract: Aging combined sewer systems in many historical cities are increasingly stressed by extreme rainfall events, which can trigger combined sewer overflows (CSO) with significant environmental and public health impacts. Forecasting the filling dynamics of overflow basins is critical for anticipating capacity exceedance and enabling timely preventive actions for CSO. We present a web-based demonstrator ( this https URL ) that integrates Deep Learning forecasting methods in both cloud and edge settings into an interactive monitoring dashboard for overflow monitoring, resilient to network outages. A video showcase is available online ( this https URL ).

analysis.tldr: 提出一个结合云和边缘深度学习预测的合流制污水溢流监测系统，支持网络中断下的弹性运行。
analysis.research_motivation: 历史城市的老化合流制污水系统因极端降雨事件面临日益严重的压力，合流制污水溢流（CSO）对环境和公共健康有重大影响，需要有效的监测和预测系统。
analysis.problem: 如何构建一个能够预测溢流池充水动态、并在网络中断时仍能保持功能的弹性监测系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 集成深度学习预测方法于云和边缘环境，构建交互式监测仪表盘，支持网络中断下的弹性运行。
contributions: 提出一个结合云和边缘的深度学习预测框架用于CSO监测。
实现了一个具有网络中断弹性的交互式监测仪表盘。
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 可能缺乏对预测模型性能的定量评估。
系统在真实场景中的长期稳定性未提及。

papers.title: LLM Jaggedness Unlocks Scientific Creativity
papers.abstract: As artificial intelligence advances, models are not improving uniformly. Instead, progress unfolds in a jagged fashion, with capabilities growing unevenly across tasks, domains, and model scales. In this work, we examine this dynamic jaggedness through the lens of scientific idea generation. We introduce SciAidanBench, a benchmark of open-ended scientific questions designed to measure the scientific creativity of large language models (LLMs). Given a scientific question, models are asked to generate as many unique and coherent ideas as possible, with the total number of valid responses serving as a proxy for creative potential. Evaluating 19 base models across 8 providers (30 total variants including reasoning versions), we find that jaggedness manifests both across models and within models. First, in a cross-task comparison between general and scientific creativity, improvements in general creativity do not translate uniformly to scientific creativity, revealing divergent capability profiles across models. Second, at the prompt level, stronger models do not improve uniformly; instead, they exhibit high variability, with bursts of creativity on some questions and limited performance on others. Third, at the domain level, individual models display uneven strengths across scientific subfields, reflecting fragmented internal capability profiles. Finally, we show that this jaggedness can be harnessed. We explore mechanisms of inference-time compute, knowledge pooling, and brainstorming to combine models effectively and construct meta-model ensembles that outperform any single model. Our results position jaggedness not as a limitation, but as a resource, a structural feature of AI progress that, when understood and leveraged, can amplify LLM-driven scientific creativity.

analysis.tldr: LLM 能力的不均匀增长（锯齿性）可以被利用来提升科学创造力，通过集成多个模型超越单一模型。
analysis.research_motivation: AI 模型在不同任务、领域和规模上的进步并不均匀，这种锯齿性通常被视为缺陷，但可能蕴含未被开发的潜力，尤其是在科学创造力这一关键领域。
analysis.problem: 如何量化和利用 LLM 在科学创意生成中的能力不均匀性（锯齿性），以提升其科学创造力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要观察到三种锯齿性表现：1) 通用创造力与科学创造力提升不一致；2) 强模型在提示层面表现高度可变；3) 单个模型在科学子领域能力不均衡。
analysis.method: 提出 SciAidanBench 基准，通过开放式科学问题评估 LLM 的科学创造力（生成独特且连贯想法的数量），并探索推理时计算、知识池化和头脑风暴等机制构建元模型集成。
contributions: 引入 SciAidanBench，一个用于衡量 LLM 科学创造力的开放式科学问题基准。
系统揭示 LLM 在科学创意生成中的锯齿性现象（跨模型、提示和领域）。
证明通过集成多个模型可以超越任何单一模型，将锯齿性转化为资源。
analysis.experiments: 评估了 8 个提供商的 19 个基础模型（共 30 个变体，包括推理版本），摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 基准可能无法覆盖所有科学领域。
集成策略的通用性和计算成本未详细讨论。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLM 能力的不均匀增长（锯齿性）可以被利用来提升科学创造力，通过集成多个模型超越单一模型。

papers.title: Deep Arguing
papers.abstract: Deep learning has become the dominant approach for creating high capacity, scalable models across diverse data modalities. However, because these models rely on a large number of learned parameters, tightly couple feature extraction with task objectives, and often lack explicit reasoning mechanisms, it is difficult for humans to understand how they arrive at their predictions. Understanding what representations emerge and why they arise from the training data remains an open challenge. We introduce Deep Arguing, a novel neurosymbolic approach that integrates deep learning with argumentation construction and reasoning for interpretable classification with different data modalities. In our approach deep neural networks construct an argumentation structure wherein data points support their assigned label and attack different ones. Using differentiable argumentation semantics for reasoning, the model is trained end-to-end to jointly learn feature representation and argumentative interactions. This results in argumentation structures providing faithful case-based explanations for predictions. Structure constraints over the argumentation graph guide learning, improving both interpretability and predictive performance. Experiments with tabular and imaging datasets show that Deep Arguing achieves performance competitive with standard baselines whilst offering interpretable argumentative reasoning.

analysis.tldr: 提出Deep Arguing，一种将深度学习与论证推理结合的神经符号方法，用于可解释分类。
analysis.research_motivation: 深度学习模型因参数多、特征提取与任务目标耦合紧密且缺乏显式推理机制，导致预测过程难以理解，亟需可解释方法。
analysis.problem: 如何让深度学习模型在保持高性能的同时，提供忠实且可解释的案例式推理过程。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: Deep Arguing：深度神经网络构建论证结构，数据点支持其分配标签并攻击其他标签；通过可微论证语义进行端到端推理，联合学习特征表示和论证交互。
contributions: 提出一种将深度学习与论证推理结合的神经符号框架，实现可解释分类。
通过论证图的结构约束引导学习，提升可解释性和预测性能。
analysis.experiments: 在表格和图像数据集上实验，性能与标准基线相当，同时提供可解释的论证推理。
limitations: 可能仅适用于分类任务，未涉及其他任务类型。
论证结构的可扩展性在复杂大规模数据上可能受限。
analysis.primary_area_en: neurosymbolic & hybrid AI systems (physics-informed, logic & formal reasoning, etc.)
analysis.category: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.sub_area: 符号推理 + 神经网络
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出Deep Arguing，一种将深度学习与论证推理结合的神经符号方法，用于可解释分类。

papers.title: Agent-First Tool API: A Semantic Interface Paradigm for Enterprise AI Agent Systems
papers.abstract: As AI agents transition from research prototypes to enterprise production systems, the tool interfaces they consume remain rooted in human-oriented CRUD paradigms. This paper identifies five fundamental architectural mismatches between conventional APIs and autonomous agent requirements: exact-identifier dependence, rendering-oriented responses, single-shot interaction assumptions, user-equivalent authorization, and opaque error semantics. We propose the Agent-First Tool API paradigm, comprising three integrated mechanisms: (1) a Six-Verb Semantic Protocol that decomposes tool interactions into search, resolve, preview, execute, verify, and recover phases; (2) a Normalized Tool Contract (NTC) providing structured decision-support metadata including confidence scores, evidence chains, and suggested next actions; and (3) a dual-layer governance pipeline combining static capability policies with dynamic risk escalation. The paradigm is implemented and validated in a production multi-tenant SaaS platform serving 85 registered tools across 6 business domains. Comparative experiments on 50 real operational tasks demonstrate that Agent-First APIs achieve 88% end-to-end task success rate versus 64% for optimized CRUD baselines (+37.5%), while reducing required human interventions by 72.7% and improving autonomous error recovery by 5.8x. We establish that the paradigm is orthogonal and complementary to transport-layer standards such as MCP, operating as the semantic application layer above existing tool discovery and invocation protocols.

analysis.tldr: 提出面向AI Agent的语义化工具API范式，通过六动词协议、标准化契约和双治理管线解决传统CRUD接口与自主Agent之间的架构不匹配问题。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有工具接口基于人类CRUD范式设计，与AI Agent的自主需求存在根本性架构不匹配，导致Agent系统在企业生产中效率低下、错误频发。
analysis.problem: 传统API的精确标识依赖、渲染导向响应、单次交互假设、用户等效授权和不透明错误语义五大问题，阻碍Agent高效、可靠地调用企业工具。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出传统API与Agent需求之间存在五种架构不匹配：精确标识依赖（Agent需模糊搜索）、渲染导向响应（返回HTML而非结构化数据）、单次交互假设（无法多步协作）、用户等效授权（缺乏细粒度权限）、不透明错误语义（Agent无法自主恢复）。
analysis.method: 提出Agent-First Tool API范式，包含三个集成机制：六动词语义协议（search/resolve/preview/execute/verify/recover）、标准化工具契约（NTC，含置信度、证据链、建议动作）和双层治理管线（静态能力策略+动态风险升级）。
contributions: 系统识别并形式化了传统API与Agent需求之间的五大架构不匹配
提出Agent-First Tool API范式，包含六动词协议、NTC和双治理管线
在生产多租户SaaS平台上验证，任务成功率提升37.5%，人工干预减少72.7%，自主错误恢复提升5.8倍
analysis.experiments: 在生产多租户SaaS平台上，使用85个注册工具（覆盖6个业务领域）进行50个真实操作任务的对比实验。Agent-First API实现88%端到端任务成功率，对比优化CRUD基线64%（+37.5%），人工干预减少72.7%，自主错误恢复提升5.8倍。
limitations: 范式依赖工具提供方实现六动词协议和NTC，可能增加初始适配成本
实验仅在单一SaaS平台进行，泛化性需更多跨平台验证

analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出面向AI Agent的语义化工具API范式，通过六动词协议、标准化契约和双治理管线解决传统CRUD接口与自主Agent之间的架构不匹配问题。

papers.title: Bridging Sequence and Graph Structure for Epigenetic Age Prediction
papers.abstract: Epigenetic clocks based on DNA methylation have emerged as powerful tools for estimating biological age, with broad applications in aging research, age-related disease studies, and longevity science. Despite advances across machine learning approaches to epigenetic age prediction, spanning penalised linear regression, deep feedforward networks, residual architectures, and graph neural networks, no existing method jointly models co-methylation graph structure and site-specific DNA sequence context within a unified framework. We propose a unified sequence--graph integration framework for epigenetic age prediction that addresses this gap, integrating eight-dimensional DNA sequence statistical features through a lightweight gated modulation mechanism that adaptively scales each site's methylation signal according to its sequence-determined biological relevance prior to graph convolution. Evaluated on 3,707 blood methylation samples against a comprehensive set of baselines, our method achieves a test MAE of 3.149 years, a 12.8\% improvement over the strongest graph-based baseline. Biologically informed statistical features outperform CNN-based sequence encoding, demonstrating that handcrafted sequence features are more effective than end-to-end learned representations in this data regime. Post-hoc interpretability analysis identifies CpG density and local adenine frequency as features with age-dependent importance shifts, consistent with known mechanisms of age-related hypermethylation at CpG-dense promoter regions. Our code is at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: 提出序列-图融合框架用于表观遗传年龄预测，结合DNA序列统计特征与共甲基化图结构，在血液样本上实现MAE 3.149年，比最强图基线提升12.8%。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有表观遗传时钟方法（如线性回归、深度网络、图神经网络）未能同时建模共甲基化图结构和位点特异性DNA序列上下文，限制了预测精度和生物学可解释性。
analysis.problem: 如何在一个统一框架中融合DNA序列统计特征与共甲基化图结构，以提升表观遗传年龄预测的准确性和可解释性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 手工设计的序列统计特征（如CpG密度、局部腺嘌呤频率）比CNN端到端学习表示更有效；CpG密度和局部腺嘌呤频率的重要性随年龄变化，与年龄相关高甲基化机制一致。
analysis.method: 提出序列-图融合框架，通过轻量级门控调制机制，根据序列统计特征自适应缩放每个位点的甲基化信号，再输入图卷积网络。
contributions: 首次在统一框架中联合建模共甲基化图结构和DNA序列上下文用于表观遗传年龄预测。
提出轻量级门控调制机制，有效融合八维序列统计特征与图结构信息。
在3707个血液样本上实现MAE 3.149年，比最强图基线提升12.8%，并揭示年龄相关特征重要性变化。
analysis.experiments: 在3707个血液甲基化样本上评估，与多种基线比较，测试MAE为3.149年，比最强图基线提升12.8%。
limitations: 仅基于血液样本，可能不适用于其他组织类型。
手工特征设计可能无法捕捉所有序列模式，泛化性需进一步验证。

analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出序列-图融合框架用于表观遗传年龄预测，结合DNA序列统计特征与共甲基化图结构，在血液样本上实现MAE 3.149年，比最强图基线提升12.8%。

papers.title: A Reflective Storytelling Agent for Older Adults: Integrating Argumentation Schemes and Argument Mining in LLM-Based Personalised Narratives
papers.abstract: This work investigates whether knowledge-driven large language model (LLM)-based storytelling can support purposeful narrative interaction with a digital companion for older adults. To address known limitations of LLMs, including hallucinations and limited transparency, we present a reflective storytelling agent integrating knowledge graphs, user modelling, argumentation theory, and argument mining to guide and inspect narrative generation. The study consisted of two phases. Phase I employed participatory design involving 11 domain experts in a formative evaluation that informed iterative refinement. The resulting system generates narratives grounded in structured user models representing health-promoting activities and motivations. Phase II involved 55 older adults evaluating persona-based narratives across four prompts and two creativity levels. Participants assessed perceived purpose, usefulness, cultural relatability, and inconsistencies. The system additionally computed hallucination-risk indicators to evaluate generated narratives. Participants recognised personally relevant purposes in roughly two thirds of narratives, while argument-based purposes were identified in around half of these cases. Cultural recognisability strongly influenced willingness to use the functionality, whereas minor inconsistencies were often tolerated when narratives remained understandable and personally relevant. Narratives with higher hallucination-risk indicators were more often perceived as inconsistent, while higher argument-quality indicators tended to co-occur with higher clarity and meaningfulness ratings. Overall, the study positions argument mining as a reflective inspection mechanism for comparing formal grounding signals with human evaluations in health-oriented LLM storytelling for older adults.

analysis.tldr: 提出一个结合知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘的反思性叙事代理，用于为老年人生成个性化故事，并通过实验验证其有效性和可接受性。
analysis.research_motivation: 老年人数字伴侣需要有意义的故事互动，但LLM存在幻觉和透明度不足的问题，现有方法缺乏可解释性和个性化。
analysis.problem: 如何为老年人构建一个可解释、个性化且能减少幻觉的LLM叙事代理。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 集成知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘，生成基于用户健康活动与动机的结构化叙事，并使用论证挖掘作为反思性检查机制。
contributions: 提出一个结合论证方案和论证挖掘的反思性叙事代理框架。
通过两阶段研究（专家参与设计和55名老年人评估）验证了方法的有效性。
analysis.experiments: 两阶段研究：第一阶段11名领域专家参与迭代设计；第二阶段55名老年人评估基于人物角色的叙事，测量感知目的、有用性、文化相关性、不一致性和幻觉风险指标。
limitations: 样本量较小（55名老年人），可能限制泛化性。
论证挖掘的自动评估与人类判断的一致性有待进一步验证。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一个结合知识图谱、用户建模、论证理论和论证挖掘的反思性叙事代理，用于为老年人生成个性化故事，并通过实验验证其有效性和可接受性。

papers.title: PrimeKG-CL: A Continual Graph Learning Benchmark on Evolving Biomedical Knowledge Graphs
papers.abstract: Biomedical knowledge graphs underwrite drug repurposing and clinical decision support, yet the upstream ontologies they depend on update on independent cycles that add millions of edges and deprecate hundreds of thousands more between releases. Yet existing continual graph learning has been studied almost exclusively on synthetic random splits of static, generic KGs, a regime that cannot reproduce the asynchronous, structured evolution real biomedical KGs undergo. To this end, we introduce PrimeKG-CL, a CGL benchmark built from nine authoritative biomedical databases (129K+ nodes, 8.1M+ edges, 10 node types, 30 relation types) with two genuine temporal snapshots (June 2021, July 2023; 5.83M edges added, 889K removed, 7.21M persistent), 10 entity-type-grouped tasks, multimodal node features, and a per-task persistent/added/removed test stratification. On three tasks (biomedical relationship prediction, entity classification, KGQA), we evaluate six CL strategies across four KGE decoders, plus LKGE, an LLM-RAG agent, and CMKL. We find that decoder choice and continual learning strategy interact strongly: no single strategy performs best across all decoders, and mismatched combinations can significantly degrade performance. Moreover, only DistMult exhibits a clear separation between persistent and deprecated knowledge, indicating that standard metrics conflate retention of still-valid facts with failure to forget outdated ones; this effect is absent under RotatE. In addition, multimodal features improve entity-level tasks by up to 60%, and a recent CKGE framework (IncDE) failed to scale to our 5.67M-triple base task across five attempts up to 350GB RAM. Data, pipeline, baselines, and the stratified split are released openly. Dataset: this http URL |Code: this http URL

analysis.tldr: 首个面向真实演化生物医学知识图谱的持续图学习基准，揭示解码器与CL策略的强交互及标准指标对过时知识遗忘的混淆。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有持续图学习研究几乎全部基于静态通用KG的合成随机划分，无法模拟真实生物医学KG异步、结构化的演化过程，亟需反映实际更新模式的基准。
analysis.problem: 构建一个基于真实生物医学知识图谱时间快照的持续图学习基准，以评估模型在关系预测、实体分类和KGQA任务上的表现。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 解码器选择与持续学习策略存在强交互，无单一策略在所有解码器上最优；仅DistMult能清晰区分持久知识与过时知识，标准指标混淆了保留有效事实与遗忘过时事实；多模态特征提升实体级任务达60%；现有CKGE框架IncDE无法扩展到5.67M三元组的基任务。
analysis.method: 从9个权威生物医学数据库构建两个真实时间快照（2021年6月、2023年7月），包含129K+节点、8.1M+边，设计10个实体类型分组任务，提供多模态节点特征和分层测试集。
contributions: 首个基于真实演化生物医学KG的持续图学习基准PrimeKG-CL，包含时间快照、多模态特征和分层测试集。
系统评估6种CL策略与4种KGE解码器在三个任务上的表现，揭示解码器与CL策略的强交互。
发现标准指标混淆持久知识与过时知识的遗忘，仅DistMult能区分，RotatE则不能。
多模态特征显著提升实体级任务，现有CKGE框架无法扩展至大规模基任务。
analysis.experiments: 在生物医学关系预测、实体分类和KGQA三个任务上评估六种持续学习策略（结合四种KGE解码器），以及LKGE（LLM-RAG agent）和CMKL。
limitations: 仅包含两个时间快照，可能不足以捕捉长期演化模式。
基准主要基于英文生物医学数据库，语言和领域覆盖有限。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 首个面向真实演化生物医学知识图谱的持续图学习基准，揭示解码器与CL策略的强交互及标准指标对过时知识遗忘的混淆。

papers.title: Consistency as a Testable Property: Statistical Methods to Evaluate AI Agent Reliability
papers.abstract: This paper establishes a rigorous measurement science for AI agent reliability, providing a foundational framework for quantifying consistency under semantically preserving perturbations. By leveraging $U$-statistics for output-level reliability and kernel-based metrics for trajectory-level stability, we offer a principled approach to evaluating agents across diverse operating conditions. Our proposal highlights the important distinction between the core capability and execution robustness of an agent, showing that minor task-level variations can induce complete strategy breakdowns despite the agent possessing the requisite knowledge for the task. We validate our framework through extensive experiments on three agentic benchmarks, demonstrating that trajectory-level consistency metrics provide far greater diagnostic sensitivity than traditional pass@1 rates. By providing the mathematical tools to isolate where and why agents deviate, we enable the identification and rectification of architectural concerns that hinder the deployment of agents in high-stakes, real-world environments.

analysis.tldr: 提出基于U统计量和核方法的统计框架，用于量化AI智能体在语义保持扰动下的输出一致性和轨迹稳定性。
analysis.research_motivation: AI智能体在高风险场景中的可靠性评估缺乏严格的度量标准，传统pass@1率无法诊断策略崩溃等细粒度问题。
analysis.problem: 如何建立可测试的统计方法，量化智能体在语义保持扰动下的一致性和鲁棒性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，即使智能体具备完成任务所需知识，微小任务级变化也可能导致完整策略崩溃，表明核心能力与执行鲁棒性存在重要区别。
analysis.method: 利用U统计量评估输出级可靠性，基于核的度量评估轨迹级稳定性，区分核心能力与执行鲁棒性。
contributions: 建立了AI智能体可靠性的严格度量科学框架
提出输出级和轨迹级两种一致性统计方法
通过实验证明轨迹级一致性指标比pass@1率具有更高诊断灵敏度
analysis.experiments: 在三个智能体基准上进行了广泛实验，验证了框架的有效性。
limitations: 可能依赖特定扰动类型的选择
计算开销可能较大
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出基于U统计量和核方法的统计框架，用于量化AI智能体在语义保持扰动下的输出一致性和轨迹稳定性。

papers.title: SLASH the Sink: Sharpening Structural Attention Inside LLMs
papers.abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) show remarkable semantic understanding but often struggle with structural understanding when processing graph topologies in a serialized format. Existing solutions rely on training external graph-based adapters or fine-tuning, which incur high costs and lost generalizability. In this work, we investigate the internal mechanisms of LLMs and present a critical finding: LLMs spontaneously reconstruct the graph's topology internally, evidenced by a distinct "sawtooth" pattern in their attention maps that structurally aligns with the "token-level adjacency matrix". However, this intrinsic structural understanding is diluted by the attention sink. We theoretically formalize this dilution as a representation bottleneck, stemming from a fundamental conflict: the model's anisotropic bias, essential for language tasks, suppresses the topology-aware local aggregation required for graph reasoning. To address this, we propose a training-free solution, named StructuraL Attention SHarpening (Slash), which amplifies this internal structural understanding via a plug-and-play attention redistribution. Experiments on pure graph tasks and molecular prediction validate Slash delivers significant and consistent performance gains across diverse LLMs.

analysis.tldr: 无需微调，通过锐化注意力图即可释放LLM内部的结构理解能力，在图上任务中取得显著提升。
analysis.research_motivation: LLM在序列化图数据上结构理解能力弱，现有方法依赖外部适配器或微调，成本高且泛化性差。
analysis.problem: 如何在不微调的情况下增强LLM对图拓扑结构的内在理解能力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: LLM内部注意力图呈现与token级邻接矩阵对齐的锯齿模式，但注意力汇聚现象稀释了这种结构理解。
analysis.method: 提出训练无关的StructuraL Attention SHarpening (Slash)，通过即插即用的注意力重分布来锐化内部结构注意力。
contributions: 发现LLM内部存在与图拓扑对齐的锯齿注意力模式，并理论化注意力汇聚导致的结构理解瓶颈。
提出无需微调的Slash方法，通过注意力锐化提升LLM在图任务上的性能。
analysis.experiments: 在纯图任务和分子预测任务上验证，Slash在不同LLM上均取得显著且一致的性能提升。
limitations: 方法仅在序列化图数据上验证，对其他结构数据（如树、超图）的适用性未知。
注意力锐化可能影响语言任务的性能，摘要未提及跨任务评估。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 无需微调，通过锐化注意力图即可释放LLM内部的结构理解能力，在图上任务中取得显著提升。

papers.title: SkillEvolver: Skill Learning as a Meta-Skill
papers.abstract: Agent skills today are static artifact: authored once -- by human curation or one-shot generation from parametric knowledge -- and then consumed unchanged, with no mechanism to improve from real use. We propose \textbf{SkillEvolver}, a lightweight, plug-and-play solution for online skill learning, in which a single meta-skill iteratively authors, deploys, and refines domain-specific skills. The learning target of SkillEvolver is the skill's prose and code, not model weights, so that the resulting artifact drops into any agent without retraining; and the meta-skill itself is just another skill, loaded through the same interface by any protocol-compliant CLI-agent. Unlike trace-distillation, the meta-skill refines only after deploying the learnt skill, such that the learning signal comes from failures another agent encounters while using it -- not from exploratory traces alone. Refinement iterations are governed by a fresh-agent overfit audit that catches possible leakage as well as deployed-skill-specific failures, including the silent-bypass mode in which a skill appears valid in content but is never invoked at runtime. On $83$ SkillsBench tasks spanning $15^{+}$ domains, SkillEvolver reaches $56.8\%$ accuracy versus $43.6\%$ for curated human skills and $29.9\%$ for the no-skill baseline; on three GPU kernel optimization tasks from KernelBench, it also raises mean speedup from $1.16$ to $1.51$ on average.

analysis.tldr: SkillEvolver 提出一种元技能，通过在线迭代优化技能文本和代码，使技能在使用中持续改进，无需重训练模型。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有智能体技能是静态的，一旦创建便无法从实际使用中改进，限制了技能的适应性和性能。
analysis.problem: 如何让智能体技能在使用过程中自动迭代优化，提升其有效性和鲁棒性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: SkillEvolver 是一个轻量级即插即用的元技能，它通过迭代编写、部署和优化领域特定技能的文本和代码来学习，学习信号来自其他智能体使用该技能时遇到的失败，而非仅探索性轨迹；优化迭代受新鲜智能体过拟合审计控制，可检测泄漏和运行时未调用等失败模式。
contributions: 提出一种元技能框架，使技能在使用中在线学习，无需重训练模型。
引入新鲜智能体过拟合审计，检测技能泄漏和静默绕过等失败模式。
在 SkillsBench 和 KernelBench 上验证了有效性，显著优于人工技能和无技能基线。
analysis.experiments: 在涵盖15+领域的83个SkillsBench任务上，SkillEvolver达到56.8%准确率，优于人工技能的43.6%和无技能基线的29.9%；在KernelBench的三个GPU内核优化任务上，平均加速比从1.16提升至1.51。
limitations: 可能依赖初始技能质量，若初始技能过差可能影响收敛。
审计机制可能增加计算开销，在实时场景中需权衡。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: SkillEvolver 提出一种元技能，通过在线迭代优化技能文本和代码，使技能在使用中持续改进，无需重训练模型。

papers.title: ASIA: an Autonomous System Identification Agent
papers.abstract: Over the years, research in system identification has provided a rich set of methods for learning dynamical models, together with well-established theoretical guarantees. In practice, however, the choice of model class, training algorithm, and hyperparameter tuning is still largely left to empirical trial-and-error, requiring substantial expert time and domain experience. Motivated by recent advances in agentic artificial intelligence, we present ASIA, a framework that delegates this iterative search to a large language model acting as an autonomous coding agent. Building on existing agentic platforms, ASIA closes the loop between hypothesis, implementation, and evaluation without human intervention, requiring only a plain-English description of the identification problem. We conduct an empirical study of ASIA on two system identification benchmarks and analyse the agent's search behaviour, the architectures and training strategies it discovers, and the quality of the resulting models. We also discuss the potential of the approach and its current limitations, including implicit test leakage, reduced methodological transparency, and reproducibility concerns.

analysis.tldr: ASIA 是一个基于 LLM 的自主系统辨识智能体，自动完成模型选择、训练和调参。
analysis.research_motivation: 系统辨识方法丰富但实际应用中模型选择、算法和超参数调优依赖人工试错，耗时且需要专家经验。
analysis.problem: 如何利用大语言模型作为自主编码智能体，自动完成系统辨识的迭代搜索过程，减少人工干预。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 基于现有智能体平台，使用大语言模型作为自主编码智能体，闭环执行假设、实现和评估，仅需自然语言描述辨识问题。
contributions: 提出 ASIA 框架，将系统辨识的迭代搜索委托给 LLM 自主智能体。
在两个系统辨识基准上进行了实证研究，分析了智能体的搜索行为、发现的架构和训练策略。
analysis.experiments: 在两个系统辨识基准上进行了实证研究，分析了搜索行为、架构和训练策略，但摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 隐式测试泄露风险
方法透明度降低
可重复性问题
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: ASIA 是一个基于 LLM 的自主系统辨识智能体，自动完成模型选择、训练和调参。

papers.title: Can Agent Benchmarks Support Their Scores? Evidence-Supported Bounds for Interactive-Agent Evaluation
papers.abstract: Interactive agent benchmarks map an agent run to a binary outcome through outcome checks. When these checks rely on surface level signals or fail to capture the agent's actual action path, they cannot reliably determine whether the run succeeded. For example, a benchmark task may ask whether Alice's shipping address was changed, while the outcome check only verifies that the agent clicked "Save." This does not guarantee that the intended state change occurred, since the agent may have modified the wrong record. Treating such a run as successful therefore makes the reported score misleading. Benchmark quality thus depends not only on task design, but also on the reliability of outcome detection. We address this problem by introducing an outcome evidence reporting layer for existing benchmarks, without modifying their tasks, agents, or evaluators. The layer performs three functions. First, before scoring, it specifies which stored artifacts are required to verify the claimed outcome for each case. Second, it applies a locked checklist to each completed run and assigns one of three evidence labels: Evidence Pass, Evidence Fail, or Unknown. Third, it reports evidence supported score bounds that quantify uncertainty arising from Unknown cases. Rather than silently counting, discarding, or hiding uncertain cases inside a single aggregate success rate, the framework keeps them explicitly visible. We evaluate the outcome evidence layer on five public benchmarks: ANDROIDWORLD, AGENTDOJO, APPWORLD, tau3 bench retail, and MINIWOB. The resulting reports separate several empirically distinct failure modes.

analysis.tldr: 提出证据支持层为交互式Agent基准提供可靠分数边界，揭示表面检查的不可靠性。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有交互式Agent基准依赖表面信号进行结果检查，无法可靠判断任务是否真正成功，导致分数误导。
analysis.problem: 如何在不修改任务、Agent或评估器的情况下，量化并报告基准结果的不确定性。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，结果检查仅验证表面信号（如点击“保存”）无法保证实际状态变更，例如可能修改了错误记录，导致成功判定不可靠。
analysis.method: 引入结果证据报告层，包含三个功能：1) 指定验证每个案例所需存储工件；2) 对每个运行应用锁定检查表并分配证据标签（证据通过、证据失败、未知）；3) 报告证据支持的分数边界，量化未知案例的不确定性。
contributions: 提出一种轻量级结果证据报告层，可附加到现有基准上，无需修改任务、Agent或评估器。
在五个公开基准上评估，揭示了多种经验上不同的失败模式。
analysis.experiments: 在ANDROIDWORLD、AGENTDOJO、APPWORLD、tau3 bench retail和MINIWOB五个公开基准上评估，报告分离了多种经验上不同的失败模式。
limitations: 可能增加评估开销，需要存储工件和手动检查表。
未知标签的处理仍需人工介入或进一步自动化。
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出证据支持层为交互式Agent基准提供可靠分数边界，揭示表面检查的不可靠性。

papers.title: LLM4Branch: Large Language Model for Discovering Efficient Branching Policies of Integer Programs
papers.abstract: Efficient branching policies are essential for accelerating Mixed Integer Linear Programming (MILP) solvers. Their design has long relied on hand-crafted heuristics, and now machine learning has emerged as a promising paradigm to automate this process. However, existing learning-based methods are often hindered by their dependence on expensive expert demonstrations and the gap between training objectives and the solver's end-to-end performance. In this work, we propose LLM4Branch, a novel framework that leverages Large Language Models (LLMs) to automate the discovery of efficient branching policies. Specifically, the discovered policy is an executable program with a program skeleton generated by the LLM and a parameter vector, which is optimized via a zeroth-order method over a few instances with their end-to-end performance feedback. Extensive experiments on standard MILP benchmarks demonstrate that LLM4Branch establishes a new state-of-the-art among CPU-based methods and achieves performance competitive with advanced GPU-based models. Codes are available at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: LLM4Branch利用大语言模型自动发现高效的分支策略程序，在MILP求解器上达到SOTA。
analysis.research_motivation: MILP求解器中高效分支策略长期依赖手工启发式，现有学习方法依赖昂贵专家演示且训练目标与求解器端到端性能存在差距。
analysis.problem: 如何自动发现与求解器端到端性能对齐的高效分支策略。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 利用LLM生成可执行程序骨架和参数向量，通过零阶优化方法在少量实例上基于端到端性能反馈优化参数。
contributions: 提出LLM4Branch框架，利用LLM自动发现高效分支策略程序。
在标准MILP基准上达到CPU方法SOTA，性能与先进GPU方法竞争。
analysis.experiments: 在标准MILP基准上进行了实验，LLM4Branch在CPU方法中达到SOTA，与GPU方法性能相当。
limitations: 可能依赖LLM生成的程序骨架质量。
零阶优化在参数空间较大时可能效率较低。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: LLM4Branch利用大语言模型自动发现高效的分支策略程序，在MILP求解器上达到SOTA。

papers.title: GuardAD: Safeguarding Autonomous Driving MLLMs via Markovian Safety Logic
papers.abstract: Multimodal large language models (MLLMs) are increasingly integrated into autonomous driving (AD) systems; however, they remain vulnerable to diverse safety threats, particularly in accident-prone scenarios. Recent safeguard mechanisms have shown promise by incorporating logical constraints, yet most rely on static formulations that lack temporally grounded safety reasoning over evolving traffic interactions, resulting in limited robustness in dynamic driving environments. To address these limitations, we propose GuardAD, a model-agnostic safeguard that formulates AD safety as an evolving Markovian logical state. GuardAD introduces Neuro-Symbolic Logic Formalization, which represents safety predicates over heterogeneous traffic participants and continuously induces them via n-th order Markovian Logic Induction. This design enables the inference of emerging and latent hazards beyond single-step observations. Rather than simply vetoing unsafe actions, GuardAD performs Logic-Driven Action Revision, where inferred safety states actively guide action refinement without modifying the underlying MLLM. Extensive experiments on multiple benchmarks and AD-MLLMs demonstrate that GuardAD substantially reduces accident rates (-32.07%) while slightly improving task performance (+6.85%). Moreover, closed-loop simulation evaluations, together with physical-world vehicle studies, further validate the effectiveness and potential of GuardAD.

analysis.tldr: GuardAD 通过马尔可夫安全逻辑为自动驾驶 MLLM 提供模型无关的安全保障，显著降低事故率。
analysis.research_motivation: 自动驾驶中的多模态大模型面临多样安全威胁，现有防护机制依赖静态逻辑，缺乏对动态交通交互的时间推理能力，鲁棒性不足。
analysis.problem: 如何为自动驾驶 MLLM 设计一种能随时间演化、推理潜在危险的安全防护机制，同时不修改底层模型。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出 GuardAD，包含神经符号逻辑形式化（将安全谓词表示为马尔可夫逻辑状态）和逻辑驱动动作修正（根据推断的安全状态主动引导动作调整）。
contributions: 提出一种模型无关的自动驾驶安全防护框架 GuardAD，将安全建模为演化中的马尔可夫逻辑状态。
引入 n 阶马尔可夫逻辑归纳，实现超越单步观测的潜在危险推理。
通过逻辑驱动动作修正，在不修改 MLLM 的前提下降低事故率并提升任务性能。
analysis.experiments: 在多个基准和自动驾驶 MLLM 上，事故率降低 32.07%，任务性能提升 6.85%；还进行了闭环仿真和实车验证。
limitations: 可能依赖高质量的安全谓词定义，泛化到未知场景有挑战。
马尔可夫阶数的选择可能影响推理效果，需手动调参。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: GuardAD 通过马尔可夫安全逻辑为自动驾驶 MLLM 提供模型无关的安全保障，显著降低事故率。

papers.title: Agentic Performance at the Edge: Insights from Benchmarking
papers.abstract: Agentic artificial intelligence (AI) is a natural fit for Internet of Things (IoT) and edge systems, but edge deployments are often constrained to models around 8 billion parameters or smaller. An important question is: How much agentic-task quality is lost when model size is constrained by memory, power, and latency budgets? To address this question, in this paper, we provide an initial empirical study considering edge-focused model scaling, general-purpose versus coder-oriented model effects, and tool-enabled execution under a fixed protocol. We introduce a domain-conditioned evaluation methodology, an implementation-grounded analysis of model-tool interactions, practical guidance for model selection under constraints, and an analysis of failure modes that reveals distinct semantic versus execution failure patterns across model families. Our core finding is that edge-agent quality is not a simple function of parameter count. Robust deployment depends on the joint design of model choice and tool workflow. Domain-conditioned analysis reveals Pareto fronts in the accuracy-latency space that can guide strategy selection based on operational priorities.

analysis.tldr: 边缘设备上的智能体性能并非仅由参数量决定，模型选择与工具工作流的联合设计更为关键。
analysis.research_motivation: 边缘设备（如IoT）部署智能体AI时受限于内存、功耗和延迟，通常只能使用80亿参数以下的模型，但模型规模缩小对智能体任务质量的影响尚不明确。
analysis.problem: 量化模型规模受限于边缘设备预算时，智能体任务性能的损失程度，并探索模型选择与工具工作流对性能的联合影响。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出领域条件评估方法，通过固定协议下的工具执行实验，分析边缘模型缩放、通用型与编码型模型效果差异，并基于实现分析模型-工具交互。
contributions: 引入领域条件评估方法用于边缘智能体基准测试
提供实现层面的模型-工具交互分析
给出约束条件下的模型选择实用指南
揭示不同模型家族中语义失败与执行失败的不同模式
analysis.experiments: 摘要未提供具体实验结果。
limitations: 实验规模可能有限，仅基于初始实证研究
未覆盖所有边缘设备场景或模型类型
analysis.primary_area_en: datasets and benchmarks
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 边缘设备上的智能体性能并非仅由参数量决定，模型选择与工具工作流的联合设计更为关键。

papers.title: Agent-X: Full Pipeline Acceleration of On-device AI Agents
papers.abstract: LLM-based agents deliver state-of-the-art performance across tasks but incur high end-to-end latency on edge devices. We introduce Agent-X, a software-only, accuracy-preserving framework that accelerates both the prefill and decode stages of on-device agent workloads. Agent-X's two key components rewrite prompts to leverage prefix caching tailored to agent-specific input-token patterns and enable LLM-free speculative decoding for fast token generation with minimal overhead. On representative agentic workloads, Agent-X achieves a 1.61x end-to-end speedup in real systems with no accuracy loss and can be seamlessly integrated into existing on-device AI agents. To the best of our knowledge, ours is the first to systematically characterize and eliminate latency bottlenecks in on-device agents.

analysis.tldr: Agent-X 通过前缀缓存和免 LLM 推测解码实现端侧 AI Agent 全流水线加速，无精度损失。
analysis.research_motivation: 基于 LLM 的 Agent 在边缘设备上端到端延迟高，现有方法未系统解决预填充和解码阶段的瓶颈。
analysis.problem: 降低端侧 AI Agent 的端到端推理延迟，同时保持精度。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: Agent-X 包含两个组件：1) 重写提示以利用针对 Agent 输入令牌模式的前缀缓存；2) 免 LLM 的推测解码，实现快速令牌生成。
contributions: 首个系统刻画并消除端侧 Agent 延迟瓶颈的工作
提出软件仅、精度保持的加速框架，实现 1.61x 端到端加速
analysis.experiments: 在代表性 Agent 工作负载上，真实系统实现 1.61x 端到端加速，无精度损失。
limitations: 可能仅适用于特定 Agent 工作负载模式
推测解码的加速效果可能受任务复杂度影响
analysis.primary_area_en: infrastructure, software libraries, hardware, systems, etc.
analysis.one_sentence_summary: Agent-X 通过前缀缓存和免 LLM 推测解码实现端侧 AI Agent 全流水线加速，无精度损失。

papers.title: Autonomous FAIR Digital Objects: From Passive Assertions to Active Knowledge
papers.abstract: Scientific knowledge on the Web is published as passive assertions and cannot decide when to validate evidence, reconcile contradictions, or update confidence as findings accumulate. Curation depends on centralised middleware and institutional continuity, but when registries close, active stewardship stops even when data remain online. We advance the concept of Autonomous FAIR Digital Objects (aFDOs) from an abstract idea to an operational model, to offer a route from passive scientific publication toward accountable, standards-aligned automation that can outlive its publishing institutions. aFDO augments FDOs with three capabilities anchored in Semantic Web standards, namely 1) a policy layer over RDF-star aligned with PROV-O, SHACL, and ODRL for portable condition-action rules, 2) an announcement layer over ActivityStreams 2.0 that bounds per-announcement evaluation cost, and 3) an agreement layer that resolves multi-source contradictions through reputation and confidence weighted agreement under a bounded adversarial model. We provide a formal definition that distinguishes policy specifications, event handlers, and communication interfaces. We evaluate an open reference implementation on 4,305 FDOs grounded in rare-disease ontologies, namely ClinVar, HPO, and Orphanet, combined with controlled synthetic observations. The consensus mechanism resolves 56.3% of 3,914 naturally occurring ClinVar conflicts where multiple submitters disagree and an expert panel has subsequently adjudicated. Under Sybil, collusion, and poisoning attacks, the mechanism degrades gracefully within its design Byzantine-tolerance bound (f < n/5), and fails as predicted beyond that bound.

analysis.tldr: 提出自治FAIR数字对象（aFDO）概念，使科学知识从被动断言转向主动知识管理，并在罕见病本体上验证了共识机制的有效性。
analysis.research_motivation: 当前科学知识以被动断言形式发布，无法自主验证证据、协调矛盾或更新置信度，依赖中心化中间件和机构持续性，但注册机构关闭后即使数据在线也会停止主动管理。
analysis.problem: 如何使科学出版物从被动断言转变为能够自主执行策略、协调矛盾并持续更新的主动知识系统。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出自治FAIR数字对象（aFDO），在FDO基础上增加三个基于语义网标准的能力层：1）基于RDF-star的策略层，结合PROV-O、SHACL和ODRL实现可移植的条件-动作规则；2）基于ActivityStreams 2.0的公告层，限制每次公告的评估成本；3）基于声誉和置信度加权的一致层，在有界对抗模型下解决多源矛盾。
contributions: 将自治FAIR数字对象从抽象概念推进到可操作模型，提供从被动科学出版到可问责、标准对齐的自动化的路径。
形式化定义了策略规范、事件处理器和通信接口，并提供了开源参考实现。
在罕见病本体（ClinVar、HPO、Orphanet）的4305个FDO上评估，共识机制解决了56.3%的自然ClinVar冲突，并在Sybil、合谋和投毒攻击下优雅退化。
analysis.experiments: 在4305个基于罕见病本体的FDO上评估，使用ClinVar、HPO、Orphanet和受控合成观测数据。共识机制解决了3914个自然ClinVar冲突中的56.3%。在Sybil、合谋和投毒攻击下，机制在设计拜占庭容错界限内优雅退化。
limitations: 共识机制仅在罕见病领域评估，泛化性未知。
依赖语义网标准，可能增加实现复杂度。
analysis.primary_area_en: other topics in machine learning (i.e., none of the above)
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出自治FAIR数字对象（aFDO）概念，使科学知识从被动断言转向主动知识管理，并在罕见病本体上验证了共识机制的有效性。

papers.title: EGL-SCA: Structural Credit Assignment for Co-Evolving Instructions and Tools in Graph Reasoning Agents
papers.abstract: Graph reasoning agents operating from natural-language inputs must solve a coupled problem: they must reconstruct a structured graph instance from text, decide whether existing computational assets are sufficient, interact with tools under a strict execution protocol, and satisfy an external verifier that checks structured correctness rather than textual plausibility. Existing approaches usually improve either the instruction side or the tool side in isolation, which leaves unclear what should be updated after failure. We propose EGL-SCA, a verifier-centric dual-space framework that models a graph reasoning agent using two collaborative components: an instruction-side policy space for reasoning strategies, and a tool-side program space for executable algorithmic tools. Our central mechanism is structural credit assignment, which maps trajectory evidence to conditional updates, precisely routing failures to either prompt optimization or tool synthesis and repair. To provide sufficient learning signals for dual-space adaptation, we introduce a training distribution stratified by task family, coupled with a Pareto-style retention strategy to balance success, generality, and parsimony. Experiments on four graph reasoning benchmarks show that EGL-SCA achieves a state-of-the-art 92.0\% average success rate. By effectively co-evolving instructions and tools, our framework significantly outperforms both pure-prompting and fixed-toolbox baselines.

analysis.tldr: 提出一种结构信用分配框架，协同进化指令与工具，在图推理Agent中达到92.0%平均成功率。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有图推理Agent方法通常孤立地改进指令或工具，失败后不清楚应更新哪一侧，缺乏协同进化机制。
analysis.problem: 如何为图推理Agent设计一种机制，在失败时准确地将错误归因于指令策略或工具程序，并协同优化两者。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出EGL-SCA，一个验证器中心的双空间框架，包含指令侧策略空间和工具侧程序空间，通过结构信用分配将轨迹证据映射到条件更新，实现失败路由到提示优化或工具合成/修复；结合按任务族分层的训练分布和Pareto保留策略。
contributions: 提出验证器中心的双空间框架，实现指令与工具的协同进化。
设计结构信用分配机制，精确路由失败原因到指令或工具更新。
引入分层训练分布和Pareto保留策略，平衡成功率、通用性和简洁性。
analysis.experiments: 在四个图推理基准上，EGL-SCA达到92.0%平均成功率，显著优于纯提示和固定工具箱基线。
limitations: 可能依赖验证器的设计质量。
Pareto保留策略的调参可能复杂。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出一种结构信用分配框架，协同进化指令与工具，在图推理Agent中达到92.0%平均成功率。

papers.title: Agent-ValueBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Agent Values
papers.abstract: Autonomous agents have rapidly matured as task executors and seen widespread deployment via harnesses such as OpenClaw. Safety concerns have rightly drawn growing research attention, and beneath them lie the values silently steering agent behavior. Existing value benchmarks, however, remain confined to LLMs, leaving agent values largely uncharted. From intuitive, empirical, and theoretical vantage points, we show that an agent's values diverge from those of its underlying LLM, and the agentic modality further introduces dataset-, evaluation-, and system-level challenges absent from text-only protocols. We close this gap with Agent-ValueBench, the first benchmark dedicated to agent values. It features 394 executable environments across 16 domains, offering 4,335 value-conflict tasks that cover 28 value systems and 332 dimensions. Every instance is co-synthesized through our purpose-built end-to-end pipeline and curated per-instance by professional psychologists. Each task ships with two pole-aligned golden trajectories whose checkpoints anchor a trajectory-level rubric-based judge. Benchmarking 14 frontier proprietary and open-weights models across 4 mainstream harnesses, we uncover three concerted findings. Agent values first manifest as a Value Tide of cross-model homogeneity beneath interpretable counter-currents. This tide bends non-additively under harness pull, and yet more decisively under deliberate steering via embedded skills. Together these results signal that the agent-alignment lever is shifting from classical model alignment and prompt steering toward harness alignment and skill steering.

analysis.tldr: 首个专门评估智能体价值观的基准，揭示智能体价值观与底层LLM的差异及新挑战。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有价值观基准局限于LLM，忽略了智能体作为任务执行者时价值观的独特性，而智能体部署日益广泛，安全需求迫切。
analysis.problem: 缺乏专门针对智能体价值观的评估基准，无法衡量智能体在真实执行环境中的价值取向。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 智能体价值观与底层LLM存在分歧；智能体模态引入数据集、评估和系统层面的新挑战；发现跨模型同质性的“价值潮”现象，且受框架和技能引导影响。
analysis.method: 构建包含394个可执行环境、4335个价值冲突任务的基准，通过端到端流水线合成实例并由心理学家逐例审核，提供轨迹级评分裁判。
contributions: 首个专门针对智能体价值观的基准Agent-ValueBench，覆盖16个领域、28种价值系统、332个维度。
揭示智能体价值观与LLM的差异，以及框架和技能引导对价值观的非线性影响。
analysis.experiments: 在14个前沿模型和4个主流框架上进行了基准测试，发现价值潮、框架拉力和技能引导等协同发现。
limitations: 基准覆盖的领域和价值系统可能仍有遗漏。
轨迹级裁判的泛化性可能受限于特定任务类型。
analysis.primary_area_en: alignment, fairness, safety, privacy, and societal considerations
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 首个专门评估智能体价值观的基准，揭示智能体价值观与底层LLM的差异及新挑战。

papers.title: How Mobile World Model Guides GUI Agents?
papers.abstract: Recent advances in vision-language models have enabled mobile GUI agents to perceive visual interfaces and execute user instructions, but reliable prediction of action consequences remains critical for long-horizon and high-risk interactions. Existing mobile world models provide either text-based or image-based future states, yet it remains unclear which representation is useful, whether generated rollouts can replace real environments, and how test-time guidance helps agents of different strengths. To answer the above questions, we filter and annotate mobile world-model data, then train world models across four modalities: delta text, full text, diffusion-based images, and renderable code. These models achieve SoTA performance on both MobileWorldBench and Code2WorldBench. Furthermore, by evaluating their downstream utility on AITZ, AndroidControl, and AndroidWorld, we obtain three findings. First, renderable code reconstruction achieves high in-distribution fidelity and provides effective multimodal supervision for data construction, while text-based feedback is more robust for online out-of-distribution (OOD) execution. Second, world-model-generated trajectories can provide transferable interaction experience in the training process and improve agents' end-to-end task performance, although these data do not preserve the original distribution. Last, for overconfident mobile agents with low action entropy, posterior self-reflection provides limited gains, suggesting that world models are more effective as prior perception or training supervision than as universal post-hoc verifiers.

analysis.tldr: 移动世界模型通过多模态未来状态预测指导GUI代理，发现文本反馈在OOD场景更鲁棒，生成轨迹可提升代理性能但后验自省对低熵代理效果有限。
analysis.research_motivation: 移动GUI代理需要可靠的动作后果预测以实现长程和高风险交互，但现有世界模型在表示形式、环境替代性和测试时指导效果上缺乏系统研究。
analysis.problem: 系统评估不同模态（文本、图像、代码）的移动世界模型在代理任务中的下游效用，并探究生成轨迹能否替代真实环境以及测试时指导如何影响不同强度的代理。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 筛选并标注移动世界模型数据，训练四种模态（delta文本、全文本、扩散图像、可渲染代码）的世界模型，并在多个基准上评估其下游效用。
contributions: 构建并训练了四种模态的移动世界模型，在MobileWorldBench和Code2WorldBench上达到SOTA。
通过下游任务评估发现：可渲染代码在分布内保真度高，文本反馈在OOD执行中更鲁棒；生成轨迹可提升代理性能但分布偏移；后验自省对低熵代理效果有限。
analysis.experiments: 在MobileWorldBench、Code2WorldBench、AITZ、AndroidControl和AndroidWorld上评估，摘要未提供具体数值。
limitations: 生成轨迹存在分布偏移，不能完全替代真实环境。
后验自省对低熵代理提升有限，世界模型作为通用事后验证器效果不佳。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 移动世界模型通过多模态未来状态预测指导GUI代理，发现文本反馈在OOD场景更鲁棒，生成轨迹可提升代理性能但后验自省对低熵代理效果有限。

papers.title: TMAS: Scaling Test-Time Compute via Multi-Agent Synergy
papers.abstract: Test-time scaling has become an effective paradigm for improving the reasoning ability of large language models by allocating additional computation during inference. Recent structured approaches have further advanced this paradigm by organizing inference across multiple trajectories, refinement rounds, and verification-based feedback. However, existing structured test-time scaling methods either weakly coordinate parallel reasoning trajectories or rely on noisy historical information without explicitly deciding what should be retained and reused, limiting their ability to balance exploration and exploitation. In this work, we propose TMAS, a framework for scaling test-time compute via multi-agent synergy. TMAS organizes inference as a collaborative process among specialized agents, enabling structured information flow across agents, trajectories, and refinement iterations. To support effective cross-trajectory collaboration, TMAS introduces hierarchical memories: the experience bank reuses low-level reliable intermediate conclusions and local feedback, while the guideline bank records previously explored high-level strategies to steer subsequent rollouts away from redundant reasoning patterns. Furthermore, we design a hybrid reward reinforcement learning scheme tailored to TMAS, which jointly preserves basic reasoning capability, enhances experience utilization, and encourages exploration beyond previously attempted solution strategies. Extensive experiments on challenging reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that TMAS achieves stronger iterative scaling than existing test-time scaling baselines, while hybrid reward training further improves scaling effectiveness and stability across iterations. Code and data are available at this https URL .

analysis.tldr: TMAS通过多智能体协同和分层记忆机制，在推理时扩展计算以提升LLM推理能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有测试时扩展方法要么弱协调并行推理轨迹，要么依赖噪声历史信息而缺乏显式保留与重用决策，难以平衡探索与利用。
analysis.problem: 如何通过多智能体协同和结构化信息流，在推理时有效扩展计算以提升LLM的推理能力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出TMAS框架，组织推理为多智能体协作过程，引入经验库和指南库两种分层记忆，并设计混合奖励强化学习方案。
contributions: 提出TMAS框架，通过多智能体协同和分层记忆实现结构化信息流。
设计混合奖励强化学习方案，提升扩展效果和稳定性。
analysis.experiments: 在多个具有挑战性的推理基准上，TMAS比现有测试时扩展基线取得更强的迭代扩展效果，混合奖励训练进一步改善扩展效果和稳定性。
limitations: 可能依赖多智能体通信开销。
分层记忆的规模可能影响效率。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: TMAS通过多智能体协同和分层记忆机制，在推理时扩展计算以提升LLM推理能力。

papers.title: PaperFit: Vision-in-the-Loop Typesetting Optimization for Scientific Documents
papers.abstract: A LaTeX manuscript that compiles without error is not necessarily publication-ready. The resulting PDFs frequently suffer from misplaced floats, overflowing equations, inconsistent table scaling, widow and orphan lines, and poor page balance, forcing authors into repetitive compile-inspect-edit cycles. Rule-based tools are blind to rendered visuals, operating only on source code and log files. Text-only LLMs perform open-loop text editing, unable to predict or verify the two-dimensional layout consequences of their changes. Reliable typesetting optimization therefore requires a visual closed loop with verification after every edit. We formalize this problem as Visual Typesetting Optimization (VTO), the task of transforming a compilable LaTeX paper into a visually polished, page-budget-compliant PDF through iterative visual verification and source-level revision, and introduce a five-category taxonomy of typesetting defects to guide diagnosis. We present PaperFit, a vision-in-the-loop agent that iteratively renders pages, diagnoses defects, and applies constrained repairs. To benchmark VTO, we construct PaperFit-Bench with 200 papers across 10 venue templates and 13 defect types at different difficulty. Extensive experiments show that PaperFit outperforms all baselines by a large margin, establishing that bridging the gap from compilable source to publication-ready PDF requires vision-in-the-loop optimization and that VTO constitutes a critical missing stage in the document automation pipeline.

analysis.tldr: 提出视觉在环的排版优化方法PaperFit，通过迭代渲染-诊断-修复将可编译LaTeX文档自动优化为出版级PDF。
analysis.research_motivation: LaTeX文档编译成功不等于出版就绪，常出现浮动体错位、公式溢出、表格缩放不一致、孤行寡行、页面不平衡等问题，作者需反复编译-检查-编辑。基于规则的工具仅操作源码和日志，无法感知渲染效果；纯文本LLM进行开环文本编辑，无法预测或验证二维布局后果。
analysis.problem: 如何将可编译的LaTeX论文自动转化为视觉上精良、符合页数预算的PDF，即视觉排版优化（VTO）问题。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出PaperFit，一个视觉在环的智能体，迭代执行页面渲染、缺陷诊断和约束修复，并引入五类排版缺陷分类法指导诊断。
contributions: 形式化定义视觉排版优化（VTO）任务，并建立五类排版缺陷分类法。
提出PaperFit视觉在环优化方法，通过迭代渲染-诊断-修复实现自动排版优化。
构建PaperFit-Bench基准，包含200篇论文、10个会议模板、13种缺陷类型及不同难度。
analysis.experiments: 在PaperFit-Bench上实验，PaperFit大幅优于所有基线。
limitations: 可能依赖LaTeX编译环境，对非LaTeX文档不适用。
修复策略可能受限于预定义缺陷类型，对罕见缺陷效果未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to computer vision, audio, language, and other modalities
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出视觉在环的排版优化方法PaperFit，通过迭代渲染-诊断-修复将可编译LaTeX文档自动优化为出版级PDF。

papers.title: CORTEG: Foundation Models Enable Cross-Modality Representation Transfer from Scalp to Intracranial Brain Recordings
papers.abstract: Intracranial electrocorticography (ECoG) offers high-signal-to-noise access to cortical activity for brain-computer interfaces, yet limited per-patient data has led most prior work to rely on small, subject-specific decoders that neglect information shared across patients. We investigate whether large pretrained scalp-EEG foundation models (EEG FMs) can be adapted to ECoG, enabling cross-patient learning and competitive decoding performance while calibrating to a held-out patient in 10-30 minutes on a single GPU. We introduce CORTEG, a cross-modality transfer framework that combines a pretrained EEG FM backbone, an electrode-aware KNNSoftFourier spatial adapter, a dual-stream tokenizer for low-frequency and high-gamma activity, and a leave-one-subject-out fine-tuning strategy. We evaluate CORTEG on two challenging regression tasks: public finger trajectory regression (n=9) and private audio envelope regression (n=16). CORTEG matches or exceeds the strongest task-specific baselines on both tasks: it reaches the highest mean correlation among compared methods on the public finger benchmark (gain not statistically significant on n=9 subjects), with larger and statistically significant gains on the audio task and in low-data per-patient calibration. Feature analyses align with neurophysiology, and latent manifolds capture low-dimensional finger-movement structure. CORTEG provides systematic evidence that scalp-EEG pretraining can be repurposed for ECoG decoding, enabling data-efficient intracranial BCIs that can adapt to new patients.

analysis.tldr: 利用预训练头皮脑电基础模型，通过跨模态迁移实现高效颅内脑电解码，支持快速跨患者适应。
analysis.research_motivation: 颅内脑电（ECoG）信号质量高但患者数据有限，传统方法依赖小规模、个体化解码器，无法利用跨患者共享信息，限制了脑机接口的实用性和泛化能力。
analysis.problem: 如何利用大规模预训练的头皮脑电（scalp-EEG）基础模型，实现从头皮到颅内脑电（ECoG）的跨模态表征迁移，以支持跨患者学习和高效解码。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要未提供具体现象分析。
analysis.method: 提出CORTEG框架，包含预训练EEG基础模型骨干、电极感知的KNNSoftFourier空间适配器、用于低频和高伽马活动的双流分词器，以及留一受试者微调策略。
contributions: 首次证明预训练头皮EEG基础模型可有效迁移至ECoG解码，实现跨患者学习。
提出CORTEG框架，在手指轨迹回归和音频包络回归任务上匹配或超越最强基线，且在低数据校准下增益显著。
analysis.experiments: 在公共手指轨迹回归（n=9）和私有音频包络回归（n=16）两个任务上评估，CORTEG在手指任务上达到最高平均相关性（统计不显著），在音频任务和低数据校准下获得显著增益。
limitations: 手指轨迹回归任务上的增益未达到统计显著性，可能受限于小样本量。
仅验证了两个回归任务，对其他ECoG解码任务（如分类）的泛化性未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to neuroscience & cognitive science
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 神经/认知科学
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 利用预训练头皮脑电基础模型，通过跨模态迁移实现高效颅内脑电解码，支持快速跨患者适应。

papers.title: EmbodiSkill: Skill-Aware Reflection for Self-Evolving Embodied Agents
papers.abstract: Embodied agents can benefit from skills that guide object search, action execution, and state changes across diverse environments. Since embodied environments vary across layouts, object states, and other execution factors, these skills must self-evolve from trajectories generated during task execution. However, existing skill self-evolution methods are mainly developed in digital environments and often convert trajectories into coarse skill updates. Directly applying this paradigm to embodied settings is problematic, because a failed task execution may reflect not only incorrect skill content, but also an execution lapse in which the agent fails to follow valid guidance. We propose EmbodiSkill, a training-free framework for embodied skill self-evolution through skill-aware reflection and targeted revision. EmbodiSkill interprets each trajectory with respect to the current skill, uses skill-changing evidence to update the skill body, and uses execution-lapse evidence to preserve and emphasize valid guidance. Experiments on ALFWorld and EmbodiedBench show that EmbodiSkill consistently improves embodied task success. On ALFWorld, EmbodiSkill enables a frozen Qwen3.5-27B executor to reach 93.28% task success, outperforming GPT-5.2 used as a direct agent without skills by 31.58%. These results show that skill-aware self-evolution helps embodied agents accumulate reusable procedural knowledge from their own trajectories.

analysis.tldr: EmbodiSkill 通过技能感知反思和定向修正，让具身代理从自身轨迹中自我进化技能，无需额外训练。
analysis.research_motivation: 具身环境多样且复杂，现有技能自我进化方法主要针对数字环境，将轨迹转化为粗粒度技能更新，直接应用于具身场景时，任务失败可能源于技能内容错误或执行失误，难以区分。
analysis.problem: 如何让具身代理从任务执行轨迹中自动识别并修正技能错误，同时保留正确的执行指导，实现技能自我进化。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 摘要指出，现有方法在数字环境中将轨迹转换为粗粒度技能更新，但在具身环境中，任务失败可能不仅因为技能内容错误，还可能因为代理未能遵循有效指导（执行失误）。
analysis.method: 提出 EmbodiSkill，一种免训练框架，通过技能感知反思和定向修正实现技能自我进化：根据当前技能解释每条轨迹，用技能改变证据更新技能主体，用执行失误证据保留并强调有效指导。
contributions: 提出 EmbodiSkill，首个针对具身代理的技能感知反思与定向修正框架，实现免训练的技能自我进化。
在 ALFWorld 和 EmbodiedBench 上验证，EmbodiSkill 持续提升任务成功率，在 ALFWorld 上使用冻结的 Qwen3.5-27B 执行器达到 93.28% 成功率，超过直接使用 GPT-5.2 作为代理的 31.58%。
analysis.experiments: 在 ALFWorld 和 EmbodiedBench 上进行实验，结果显示 EmbodiSkill 持续提升具身任务成功率。具体地，在 ALFWorld 上，使用冻结的 Qwen3.5-27B 执行器达到 93.28% 任务成功率，比直接使用 GPT-5.2 作为代理（无技能）高出 31.58%。
limitations: 可能依赖基础模型的能力，对于弱基础模型效果可能有限。
实验仅在模拟环境（ALFWorld、EmbodiedBench）中验证，真实世界泛化性未知。
analysis.primary_area_en: applications to robotics, autonomy, planning
analysis.primary_area: 应用: 机器人/自动化/规划
analysis.one_sentence_summary: EmbodiSkill 通过技能感知反思和定向修正，让具身代理从自身轨迹中自我进化技能，无需额外训练。

papers.title: Verifiable Process Rewards for Agentic Reasoning
papers.abstract: Reinforcement learning from verifiable rewards (RLVR) has improved the reasoning abilities of large language models (LLMs), but most existing approaches rely on sparse outcome-level feedback. This sparsity creates a credit assignment challenge in long-horizon agentic reasoning: a trajectory may fail despite containing many correct intermediate decisions, or succeed despite containing flawed ones. In this work, we study a class of densely-verifiable agentic reasoning problems, where intermediate actions can be objectively checked by symbolic or algorithmic oracles. We propose Verifiable Process Rewards (VPR), a framework that converts such oracles into dense turn-level supervision for reinforcement learning, and instantiate it in three representative settings: search-based verification for dynamic deduction, constraint-based verification for logical reasoning, and posterior-based verification for probabilistic inference. We further provide a theoretical analysis showing that dense verifier-grounded rewards can improve long-horizon credit assignment by providing more localized learning signals, with the benefit depending on the reliability of the verifier. Empirically, VPR outperforms outcome-level reward and rollout-based process reward baselines across controlled environments, and more importantly, transfers to both general and agentic reasoning benchmarks, suggesting that verifiable process supervision can foster general reasoning skills applicable beyond the training environments. Our results indicate that VPR is a promising approach for enhancing LLM agents whenever reliable intermediate verification is available, while also highlighting its dependence on oracle quality and the open challenge of extending VPR to less structured, open-ended environments.

analysis.tldr: 提出可验证过程奖励（VPR）框架，利用符号或算法预言机为智能体推理提供密集的中间步骤监督信号，提升长程信用分配与泛化能力。
analysis.research_motivation: 现有RLVR方法主要依赖稀疏的结果级反馈，在长程智能体推理中面临信用分配难题：轨迹可能因包含许多正确中间步骤而失败，或因包含错误步骤而成功。
analysis.problem: 如何为长程智能体推理提供密集、可验证的中间步骤奖励信号，以改善信用分配并提升推理能力。
analysis.phenomenon_analysis: 稀疏结果奖励导致信用分配困难；密集可验证奖励可提供更局部的学习信号，其收益取决于验证器的可靠性。
analysis.method: 提出Verifiable Process Rewards (VPR)框架，将符号或算法预言机转化为密集的回合级监督信号，用于强化学习；在搜索验证、约束验证和后验验证三种场景中实例化。
contributions: 提出VPR框架，利用可验证预言机为智能体推理提供密集过程奖励。
在三种代表性推理场景中实例化VPR，并提供理论分析证明密集验证奖励可改善长程信用分配。
实验表明VPR在受控环境及通用/智能体推理基准上优于结果级奖励和基于rollout的过程奖励基线。
analysis.experiments: 在受控环境及通用和智能体推理基准上进行实验，VPR优于结果级奖励和基于rollout的过程奖励基线，并展现出向训练环境外泛化的能力。
limitations: 依赖验证器质量，低质量验证器可能限制收益。
当前主要适用于可结构化验证的环境，扩展到非结构化开放环境仍是开放挑战。
analysis.primary_area_en: foundation or frontier models, including LLMs
analysis.one_sentence_summary: 提出可验证过程奖励（VPR）框架，利用符号或算法预言机为智能体推理提供密集的中间步骤监督信号，提升长程信用分配与泛化能力。

papers.title: Positive Alignment: Artificial Intelligence for Human Flourishing
